paragraph: New Milford's single-family market is moving at a real seller's pace right now. Fifty-three homes sold over seven months at a median of $680,000, with over 56 percent selling above asking and a sale-to-list ratio of 1.01. The median days on market of 14 tells you priced homes aren't lingering. You're looking at a competitive but not frenzied market where sellers can afford to wait for the right offer rather than chase volume with aggressive pricing. The range is wide, from $501,000 to $1,900,000, which reflects New Milford's mix of smaller starter homes and larger estates on the same market. If you're buying here, the homes that arrive on the market in good condition will draw interest quickly. Pricing below asking to manufacture urgency isn't the local play. Come in at market value or slightly above it for the home you want, understand you might be one of several offers, and be ready to move when the right property hits.
